reasons: Lolita can protect teammates from many ranged pressure patterns and reduce Popol's lane poke value.
Her shield and frontline presence make it harder for Popol to convert Kupa pressure into safe damage.

proof.summary: Popol and Kupa wants to pressure through ranged attacks and Kupa control, while Lolita can block incoming projectile pressure from the front and protect nearby allies.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces the correct direction before Popol commits damage.
Teammates play behind the shield instead of splitting.
failureCases: Kupa locks Lolita or a carry before the shield is ready.
Lolita wastes shield before the real trade.

reasons: Natalia can punish Popol directly if she bypasses Kupa and attacks from fog.
Popol is much weaker when the assassin reaches him instead of fighting through Kupa first.

proof.summary: Popol wants Kupa and traps to control approach paths, while Natalia can use camouflage and bush pressure to create a direct backline pickoff threat.
worksBestWhen: Natalia tracks trap placement and avoids predictable paths.
She waits until Kupa is away, low, or committed forward.
failureCases: Natalia walks into traps or Kupa peel.
Natalia cannot finish the burst after revealing.

reasons: Clint can contest Popol's lane pressure with high-damage ranged poke and skill-enhanced trades.
He can punish Popol when Popol steps forward behind Kupa.

proof.summary: Popol wants to pressure lane through Kupa and traps, while Clint can answer with long-range poke and bursty trade patterns from safer spacing.
worksBestWhen: Clint keeps wave control and avoids trap angles.
Clint pokes Popol instead of wasting everything on Kupa.
failureCases: Kupa locks Clint before he can trade back.
Clint is forced into trap-controlled bushes.
Popol gets early lane tempo and freezes Clint out.

reasons: Lesley can play for patient long-range trades and punish Popol when he lacks direct access.
True-damage poke can pressure Popol without requiring extended basic-attack uptime into Kupa.

proof.summary: Popol wants controlled lane fights around Kupa, while Lesley can look for longer-range poke windows and disengage from direct trades.
worksBestWhen: Lesley farms safely until her poke becomes threatening.
failureCases: Kupa reaches Lesley before she can disengage.
Lesley is trapped or revealed before camouflage spacing matters.

reasons: Lancelot can bypass Kupa and reach Popol if he has dash paths.
Popol is vulnerable when Kupa is down, misplaced, or already committed forward.

proof.summary: Popol wants opponents to fight through Kupa first, while Lancelot can use dash mobility and invulnerability timing to reach Popol directly.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits until Kupa is out of position.
There are minions or targets for dash resets.
failureCases: Kupa locks Lancelot before he reaches Popol.
Lancelot lacks follow-up damage after entry.
